items.rationale: His single highest-conviction thesis: stablecoins have found product-market fit, are uncorrelated with crypto trading (organic use), are the cheapest way to send a dollar, and present the first credible path to onboard a billion people and sustain dollar dominance.

citations.quote: Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
stablecoins have found product-market fit, the infrastructure is reliable, and regulatory clarity is finally coming
Another all-time high for adjusted stablecoin volume in October, with notable breakout growth on @solana

items.rationale: Believes AI agents need an economy and that crypto may be the only viable payment rail for them; sees this as one of the clearest crypto use cases since Bitcoin, with micropayments as a natural fit.

citations.quote: Stablecoin payments and AI agents are some of the clearest use cases for crypto since Bitcoin's store of value.
Crypto might be the only viable payment rail for AI agents. Will be an exciting category to watch.
Micropayments are not being talked about enough... and AI agents are the perfect customers.

items.rationale: Sees the crypto x AI intersection as 'more clear than ever' and decentralized compute as shaping the future of AI; explicitly enthusiastic about projects like Gensyn and EigenCloud.

citations.quote: The work being done on decentralized compute will shape the future of AI.
The opportunity at the intersection of crypto and AI is more clear than ever.

items.rationale: Expects mainstream users to demand financial privacy and is excited about ZK proofs moving from theory to practical crypto applications; coined 'privacy zeason' and praised Aleo.

citations.quote: As crypto goes mainstream, people are going to expect financial privacy. Excited about everything Aleo is working on!
just adding a z before your words can start a global privacy movement. Privacy zeason is here.

items.rationale: Tracks rising DEX-to-CEX share as a sign of DeFi maturation; encouraged that 20% of spot crypto trading now happens onchain after only ~5 years.

citations.quote: DEX to CEX spot trade volume: +51%... The increasing ratio highlights the overall development of the DeFi ecosystem.
20% is pretty remarkable given DeFi has only been around for ~5 years.

items.rationale: Views 2025 institutional adoption as categorically different — large financial institutions making real commitments with massive distribution, not just exploring; tracks growing ETP net flows.

citations.quote: The large financial institutions aren't just exploring crypto, they're making real commitments.
The institutional adoption of crypto in 2025 is unlike anything we've ever seen before... These companies have massive distribution.

items.rationale: One of his favorite metrics; repeatedly celebrates all-time highs and improving account-abstraction/embedded-wallet infra (EIP-7702, Privy, Turnkey, Dynamic), calling now the best time to build a next-gen mobile wallet.

citations.quote: Wallet infrastructure has significantly improved... Now is a better time than ever to build a next-generation mobile wallet.
Another month, another all time high for mobile wallet users.

items.rationale: Sees gaming as the product most obviously suited to stablecoin microtransactions; excited that AI/vibe-coding democratizes game creation and that blockchains can coordinate IP licensing across a long tail of games (Oncade, Launcher).

citations.quote: Games are perhaps the product most obviously suited to stablecoin-based payment rails – driving billions of microtransactions annually
New AI tools and vibe coding platforms could have a profound impact on the gaming industry

items.rationale: Long-running thesis that the explosion of AI content needs an open, global coordination layer for IP, authenticity, and curation that blockchains can provide; consistently championed Story Protocol.
items.age_note: Core thesis from mid-2024; restated through gaming-IP context June 2025, so still current though less prominent than stablecoins.

citations.quote: We desperately need an open, global coordination layer for IP, and I couldn't be more excited about what Story is building!
We need an open, global coordination layer in order for AI generated content to thrive. And this is exactly what blockchains can provide.

items.rationale: Cites DePIN as an emerging category to watch and praises Helium as a pioneer now reaching millions of daily users; ties it to his broader 'world needs more decentralization' view.

citations.quote: Helium has been a pioneer in DePIN. It's incredible to see the network now reach millions of users every day.
emerging ones like perps, prediction markets, and DePIN take shape

items.rationale: Strong early believer that prediction markets surface real-time 'truth' better than polls and that broader (US) participation improves accuracy; now lists them as an emerging category taking shape.
items.age_note: Most intensive commentary was 2024 (election cycle); reaffirmed as an emerging category to watch in Oct 2025.

citations.quote: Prediction markets have proven to be a powerful tool for surfacing real time information.
emerging ones like perps, prediction markets, and DePIN take shape

items.rationale: Highlights $30B in RWAs now tokenized onchain as a marker of maturation and a category he expects to keep growing.

citations.quote: $30B in real-world assets are tokenized onchain

items.rationale: Newly names perps among emerging onchain categories he is looking forward to watching take shape.

citations.quote: to see emerging ones like perps, prediction markets, and DePIN take shape

items.rationale: Argued web proofs are an underrated category critical to bringing a billion people onchain by importing contributors with incentives exogenous to the protocol.
items.age_note: Heavily promoted in mid-2024; not explicitly restated in 2025, though consistent with his ongoing onboarding/privacy themes. Treat as persisted.

citations.quote: I'm now convinced that web proofs/zkTLS will play a critical role in getting us there. It's all about incentives.
zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto.

items.rationale: Sees a live decentralized-social experiment with vibrant developer ecosystems around open protocols like Farcaster, and speculates about open data/identity protocols built on it.
items.age_note: Stated in 2024 / early 2025; included in his 2025 list of evolving categories but with less emphasis than payments-related themes.

citations.quote: The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.

items.rationale: Worries that AI's compute, infrastructure and revenue have consolidated into a handful of companies (the 'Magnificent 7'), with the rest of the S&P 500 barely growing — framing decentralization as the needed counterforce.

citations.quote: The compute resources AI is trained with, the infrastructure AI runs on, and the revenue that AI generates has consolidated into a handful of big companies.
This consolidation is worrisome. Crypto might be the only credible counterbalancing force out there.

items.rationale: Concerned the national debt is growing out of control with falling demand for it (foreign central banks now hold more gold than US Treasuries) — a macro risk he frames stablecoins as helping address.

citations.quote: The U.S. national debt is growing out of control, and the demand for that debt is waning.
For the first time in 30 years, foreign central banks hold more reserves in gold than U.S. Treasuries.

items.rationale: Flags ~$750B of Bitcoin sitting in quantum-vulnerable addresses and the need for blockchain communities to transition to quantum-secure cryptography, though he notes the timeline is unclear.

citations.quote: Today, $750 billion worth of Bitcoin sits in quantum vulnerable addresses.
even work toward quantum resistance

items.rationale: Sees doubling of wholesale CBDC development since the Ukraine sanctions as a threat to the dollar's reserve-currency power, preferring stablecoins over a US sovereign digital currency.
items.age_note: Stated Dec 2024 and not directly restated since, though it underpins his still-current 'stablecoins sustain dollar dominance' argument. Treat as persisted.

citations.quote: wholesale CBDC developments have DOUBLED. This is a national security concern, since the dollar's status as a reserve currency gives the United States tremendous power.

items.rationale: Notes total transaction fees fell 43% year-over-year, an ambiguous signal — partly intentional fee reduction, but he wants overall blockspace demand to grow even as unit costs stay low.

citations.quote: Total transaction fees (demand for blockspace): -43%
Ideally, the overall demand (total transaction fees) grows, while gas fees... remain low.

items.rationale: Raised the risk that embedding wallets too deeply into the app stack over-abstracts blockchains away and becomes misaligned with the web3 vision — posed as an open question rather than a firm stance.
items.age_note: Posed as a debate prompt in mid-2024 and never resolved; low confidence it reflects a settled current concern.

citations.quote: There is a real risk of over-abstracting wallets and blockchains from users. Embedding wallets too deeply into the application stack is misaligned with the vision of web3.

items.rationale: One of the two dominant stablecoin issuers in what he calls a 'two horse race'; he highlighted issuer Circle's NYSE IPO as a sign of stablecoins' institutional arrival.

citations.quote: On the issuer side, it's a two horse race between USDC and Tether.
USDC issuer Circle went public on the New York Stock Exchange

items.rationale: The other dominant stablecoin issuer in the 'two horse race'; central to the stablecoin growth story he champions, with Tron a leading settlement chain.

citations.quote: On the issuer side, it's a two horse race between USDC and Tether.

items.rationale: Credits Solana as a major contributor to making blockchains fast, cheap and reliable, and repeatedly notes breakout stablecoin growth on it; also driving recent active-user spikes.

citations.quote: Another all-time high for adjusted stablecoin volume in October, with notable breakout growth on @solana
Solana has been a huge contributor to this scaling narrative

items.rationale: Cites Base (alongside high-throughput L1s) as the infrastructure that finally made stablecoins a good payments product, and notes notable recent stablecoin growth on it.

citations.quote: new Ethereum L2s like Base, we've finally been able to make stablecoins a good product for payments
we are seeing some notable growth on chains like Solana, Arbitrum, and Base.

items.rationale: Notes Ethereum (with Tron) continues to dominate stablecoin infrastructure and that USDC on Ethereum L2s now costs under a penny; also a primary ETP-flow asset.

citations.quote: On the infrastructure side, Ethereum and Tron continue to dominate.
USDC transfers on Ethereum L2s now cost less than a penny.

items.rationale: Identifies Tron as co-dominant with Ethereum on stablecoin settlement infrastructure — central rails for the stablecoin volumes he tracks.

citations.quote: On the infrastructure side, Ethereum and Tron continue to dominate.

items.rationale: Names Arbitrum among the chains showing notable recent stablecoin growth, part of the improving L2 infrastructure enabling cheap payments.

citations.quote: we are seeing some notable growth on chains like Solana, Arbitrum, and Base.

items.rationale: While bullish on Bitcoin as store of value and ETP flows, he flags a specific risk: ~$750B sits in quantum-vulnerable addresses, requiring an eventual transition to quantum-secure cryptography.
items.age_note: Concern is narrow (quantum security), not a bearish view on Bitcoin overall; stated Oct 2025.

citations.quote: Today, $750 billion worth of Bitcoin sits in quantum vulnerable addresses.

risk_regime.rationale: His most recent statements are emphatically constructive: he frames 2025 as the year crypto 'grew up' — institutions arriving, stablecoin product-market fit, reliable infrastructure, and incoming regulatory clarity — and says he's 'super excited for crypto's next phase' as 'the pieces are all finally coming together.' His enthusiasm is broad-based and structural rather than tactical, tempered only by macro (US debt) and centralization/quantum concerns that he largely frames as opportunities for crypto.

notes: View is strongly weighted to his Oct–Nov 2025 State of Crypto commentary, which is consistent with (and an intensification of) his 2024 theses rather than a reversal. The throughline across the whole corpus is unwavering: stablecoins as the killer app and on-ramp for a billion users, plus crypto-as-infrastructure for the AI era (agentic payments, decentralized compute, IP coordination, privacy). No major reversals detected; older single-stated views (zkTLS, decentralized social, CBDC concern, wallet over-abstraction) are marked persisted with age notes. He is a data/metrics-driven analyst (member of a16z crypto's research team) rather than a trader, so his 'tokens' enthusiasm centers on infrastructure chains and stablecoin issuers, not speculative assets; he explicitly likens meme coins to gambling (2025-01-20). Conviction scores reflect intensity and recency of expressed enthusiasm, not price predictions.
